title: The TRUE Origin Of Christmas Revealed
summary: This interview segment discusses the theory that the origin of Santa Claus and Christmas traditions derives from Sami people in Lapland who consumed Amanita muscaria mushrooms during winter solstice rituals. The discussion connects various elements of the Santa Claus mythology to Sami shamanic practices involving reindeer, chimneys, and red and white clothing.

key_claims.claim: The Amanita muscaria mushroom grows under trees in Lapland where the Sami people live
Sami shamans would travel with reindeer to collect Amanita muscaria mushrooms and dry them over a fireplace
The Sami people would consume these mushrooms and perform a ritual of death during the winter solstice
The Sami lived in teepees with a hole in the center and sometimes had to enter through the chimney due to snow
The Sami wore red and white clothing and shoes resembling elf shoes
The Santa Claus tradition is probably eight to ten thousand years old
